title: Warm Springy Northern Irish Jumbo Shrimp Yorkshire Pudding
description: A traditional Northern Irish dish featuring jumbo shrimp in a light and airy Yorkshire pudding, infused with herbal flavors, perfect for a spring evening. This recipe is ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly and made in one pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, milk, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Add the chopped parsley and dill to the bowl and stir to combine.
Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat and add the jumbo shrimp, cooking until pink and fully cooked.
Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set aside, leaving the butter in the skillet.
Pour the Yorkshire pudding batter into the skillet and cook for 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, or until the pudding is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and top the Yorkshire pudding with the cooked jumbo shrimp.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste, then serve hot.
